title: Add continue_as_new SDK binding and NIF
purpose: The subscription lifecycle example (DX-024) calls workflow.continue_as_new which does not exist in the aion_flow SDK. Continue-as-new is essential for long-running workflows to bound event history growth — without it, workflows accumulate unbounded history.
task: Add the continue_as_new binding to the aion_flow Gleam SDK and implement the backing NIF that records the ContinueAsNew event and terminates the current run.

requirements.title: SDK binding
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL export continue_as_new(input: a) -> Result(Never, WorkflowError) from aion/workflow that serializes the input and calls the backing NIF.
acceptance: aion_flow exports continue_as_new from the aion/workflow module.
The function signature is continue_as_new(input: a) -> Result(Never, WorkflowError).
The input is serialized before being passed to the NIF.
The function calls the backing continue_as_new NIF.
gleam build succeeds with the new binding.

requirements.title: NIF implementation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement continue_as_new as a NIF that records a ContinueAsNew event through the Recorder and signals the process to terminate with the continuation input.
acceptance: A continue_as_new NIF is registered and callable from Gleam.
The NIF records a ContinueAsNew event through the Recorder.
The NIF signals the current workflow process to terminate.
The continuation input is preserved in the ContinueAsNew event payload.
The NIF does not allow continue_as_new from within an activity context.

requirements.title: Engine handling
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL handle the ContinueAsNew termination by starting a new run of the same workflow type with the provided input and a fresh event history.
acceptance: The engine detects ContinueAsNew process termination.
The engine starts a new run of the same workflow type.
The new run receives the continuation input from the ContinueAsNew event.
The new run starts with a fresh event history (no events from the previous run).
The previous run's events remain in the store for auditability.
The content-hash module namespacing invariant is preserved (same version module is used).

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the Event enum without updating the store conformance suite.
SHALL NOT allow continue_as_new from within an activity.
SHALL NOT break the content-hash module namespacing invariant.
verification: cargo test passes for the NIF and engine modules.
gleam build succeeds for the DX-024 subscription example.
Integration test demonstrates a workflow continuing as new with fresh history.
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
